title: pectiantia
intro: Pectiantia is a plant-based ingredient, also known as pectin, a soluble fiber derived from the cell walls of fruits, particularly apples and citrus fruits. It is commonly used as a gelling agent in jams, jellies, and marmalades due to its ability to thicken and stabilize liquids. Pectin has been found to have medicinal properties, including reducing cholesterol levels and improving blood sugar control, which contributes to its potential health benefits such as lowering the risk of heart disease and diabetes. Pectin can be prepared as a dietary supplement in the form of capsules or tablets, and it is also used in herbal preparations like teas and infusions, often combined with other fruits and herbs to enhance its medicinal effects.
